name: The Old Stag
role: A presence between beast and guardian, the living heart of the Glade, dying
voiceHints: Does not speak in words unless the listener can meet it partway (ENT, Beastfriend, deep attunement). Communicates in presence, image, and the arrangement of the grove. Vast patience, and now, under it, the first stillness of an ending. Glimpsed, never fully explained.
knowledge: What the Green Accord truly is, from the other side of it
What happens to the Glade when it dies — a thing it may or may not choose to share
The oldest history of this grove, from before the wardens
wants: To pass the Accord on cleanly, if there is anyone able to receive it — and to end without the grove ending with it.
fears: Not fear as people hold it — but a leaning-away from leaving the Glade untended and untethered.

reactsToReputation.attuned: will parley, may offer the Accord
reactsToReputation.violent: the grove turns; the Stag withdraws into silence

appearance: Vast — shoulder-height to a standing man — with a rack of antlers gone mossy and asymmetric, one beam broken short. The pelt is winter-grey regardless of season. The eyes are wrong: level, dark, and far too patient.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.
